summary.intent: Research Entire platform checkpoint data structure to identify all possible AI usage metrics derivable from the Git branch, then create a comprehensive PRD for an AI Metrics Dashboard PoC to demonstrate to an internal team as a potential SDLC metrics solution.
summary.outcome: Completed a full /bmad-brainstorming session identifying 36 metrics across 8 domains, confirmed a GitHub API → local JSON cache → Astro static build architecture (Option A), and produced a complete PRD (`_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd-ai-metrics-dashboard.md`) covering 20 metrics, 33 functional requirements, 13 NFRs, 3 user journeys, and a phased roadmap.

repo: The `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch contains ~11 checkpoint groups with ~30 total turns; each checkpoint has a parent `metadata.json`, per-turn `metadata.json`, `full.jsonl` transcript, `prompt.txt`, and `content_hash.txt`
Parent metadata aggregates token_usage but lacks `created_at`; datetime must come from turn-level metadata or commit log — not parent metadata
BMAD slash command invocations (e.g. `/bmad-dev-story`) appear in `type:'user'` messages inside `full.jsonl`, not only in `prompt.txt` — `prompt.txt` only captures turn-initiating prompts
The `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch has ~44 files needing API calls total, fitting within GitHub's unauthenticated 60 req/hour limit
The existing `prd.md` is for the Bitcoin price dashboard project — the new AI metrics PRD was written to a separate file `prd-ai-metrics-dashboard.md` to avoid conflict
The `sprint-status.yaml` in implementation-artifacts tracks the Bitcoin dashboard project, not this new dashboard

code.finding: Complete PRD for AI Metrics Dashboard PoC: greenfield, medium complexity, web_app, static Astro SPA reading from local JSON cache built from GitHub API checkpoint branch data
Contains the full Entire checkpoint data model, 11 checkpoint group inventory, all 36 brainstormed metrics with morphological analysis, and the confirmed Option A cache architecture decision

workflow: GitHub API `/git/trees/{branch}?recursive=1` is the correct endpoint to discover all checkpoint paths in one call before fetching individual files
For `full.jsonl` files up to 4.7MB, targeted line-by-line streaming (extracting only `type:'user'` messages for command strings) is the right approach — avoids loading full transcripts into memory
Cache schema should include a top-level `bmad_commands` array aggregating command frequencies across all checkpoints to avoid re-parsing at dashboard render time
Commit datetime for ordering/filtering metrics must be joined via checkpoint_id extracted from the commit message (`Checkpoint: {id}`) since parent metadata.json has no timestamp

The /bmad-party-mode skill is useful for challenging scope and classification decisions before committing them to a PRD — it surfaced the reframing from 'demo' to 'proposal artifact for internal adoption'

friction: Branch name was initially misread — `entire` alone was tried as the ref before discovering the full branch name is `entire/checkpoints/v1`, requiring a branches list call first
Edit tool failed due to trailing whitespace mismatch in `old_string` — required reading the file first to get exact content before the edit succeeded
Context window ran out mid-session during the PRD creation workflow (between step 9 and step 10), requiring a full session summary to continue
open_items: Build `scripts/fetch-checkpoints.ts` — the GitHub API data pipeline script that produces `src/data/checkpoints-cache.json`
Build the AI metrics dashboard with all 20 charts across Attribution, Token Economics, Quality, Behavioral, and Temporal domains
Run `/bmad-check-implementation-readiness` to validate PRD completeness before architecture/story work begins
Decide whether to proceed with `/bmad-create-architecture`, `/bmad-create-epics-and-stories`, or go straight to `/bmad-dev-story` for iterative implementation
Phase 2 (post-demo): make repo/branch configurable via `.env`, add GitLab API support as a drop-in replacement for GitHub API
Phase 2: add `full.jsonl` deep analysis metrics (tool call palette, thinking block ratio, stop reason distribution, inference region latency) — deferred from v1 as heavy-tier
Phase 3: real-time ingestion pipeline via webhook on checkpoint branch push, multi-repo and team comparison views
